title: asterocephalus maritimus
intro: Asterocephalus maritimus, also known as sea lavender, is a flowering plant native to the coastal regions of Europe, North Africa, and the Mediterranean. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of sea lavender include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce anxiety and stress, and act as a natural antihistamine to alleviate allergy symptoms. Sea lavender is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be applied topically to the skin in the form of creams and salves.
